title: Carmelagiri Elephant Park
tagline: Carmelagiri Elephant Park — heritage city of Kerala

overview.short: Carmelagiri Elephant Park is a private park for elephant riding through the hilly paths of Munnar forest, a part of Kerala tourism, India.
overview.description: Carmelagiri Elephant Park is a private park for elephant riding through the hilly paths of Munnar forest, a part of Kerala tourism, India.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Mattupetty Dam
topPlaces.description: Mattupetty Dam (Madupetty Dam), Kannan Devan Hills in Idukki District, is a storage concrete gravity dam built in the mountains of Kerala, India, to conserve water for hydroelectricity. It has been a vital source of power and huge revenue for Idukki District.

topPlaces.name: Devikulam taluk
topPlaces.description: Devikulam taluk is one of the 5 taluks in Idukki district of the Indian state of Kerala. Devikulam taluk comprises 13 villages.

topPlaces.name: Devikulam
topPlaces.description: Devikulam is a Grama Panchayat and small hill station about 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) away from Munnar in the Idukki District of Kerala, India. It lies about 1,800 metres (5,900 ft) above sea level.

topPlaces.name: Subramanya Temple, Munnar
topPlaces.description: Sri Subramanya Temple is a Hindu temple located in Munnar in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. Lord Muruga is the principal deity.

topPlaces.name: Munnar
topPlaces.description: Munnar (Malayalam pronunciation: [muːn̪ːɐːr]) is a town located in the Idukki district of the southwestern Indian state of Kerala. Munnar is situated at around 1,600 metres (5,200 ft) above mean sea level, in the Western Ghats mountain range.

topPlaces.name: Mount Carmel Church, Munnar
topPlaces.description: The Mount Carmel Basilica of Munnar is a Catholic basilica and pilgrimage centre in the plantation town of Munnar, in the southern Indian state of Kerala. The church is known for its wood-framed windows and a statue of Christ and the Virgin Mary.

topPlaces.name: R. A. Headworks
topPlaces.description: R. A. Headworks or Ramaswamy Aiyar Headworks is a masonry type weir situated in Munnar panchayath of Munnar village in Idukki district of Kerala, India impounding mudirapuzha river. It is also called as Munnar Headworks.

topPlaces.name: Devimala
topPlaces.description: Devimala, is one of the high peaks of Western Ghats in the (Devikulam) taluk of Idukki district of Kerala. Devimala Peak lies in the Anamalai hills of Kerala.

items.note: Mattupetty Dam (Madupetty Dam), Kannan Devan Hills in Idukki District, is a storage concrete gravity dam built in the mountains of Kerala, India, to conserve water for hydroelectricity. It has been a vital source of power and huge revenue for Idukki District.
Devikulam taluk is one of the 5 taluks in Idukki district of the Indian state of Kerala. Devikulam taluk comprises 13 villages.
Devikulam is a Grama Panchayat and small hill station about 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) away from Munnar in the Idukki District of Kerala, India. It lies about 1,800 metres (5,900 ft) above sea level.
Sri Subramanya Temple is a Hindu temple located in Munnar in Idukki district in the Indian state of Kerala. Lord Muruga is the principal deity.

Munnar (Malayalam pronunciation: [muːn̪ːɐːr]) is a town located in the Idukki district of the southwestern Indian state of Kerala. Munnar is situated at around 1,600 metres (5,200 ft) above mean sea level, in the Western Ghats mountain range.

The Mount Carmel Basilica of Munnar is a Catholic basilica and pilgrimage centre in the plantation town of Munnar, in the southern Indian state of Kerala. The church is known for its wood-framed windows and a statue of Christ and the Virgin Mary.

R. A. Headworks or Ramaswamy Aiyar Headworks is a masonry type weir situated in Munnar panchayath of Munnar village in Idukki district of Kerala, India impounding mudirapuzha river. It is also called as Munnar Headworks.
Devimala, is one of the high peaks of Western Ghats in the (Devikulam) taluk of Idukki district of Kerala. Devimala Peak lies in the Anamalai hills of Kerala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Carmelagiri Elephant Park?
faq.a: The best time to visit Carmelagiri Elephant Park is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Carmelagiri Elephant Park?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (77 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Carmelagiri Elephant Park?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Carmelagiri Elephant Park at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Carmelagiri Elephant Park?
faq.a: Stays in Carmelagiri Elephant Park start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Carmelagiri Elephant Park?
faq.a: The top attractions are Mattupetty Dam, Devikulam taluk, Devikulam, Subramanya Temple, Munnar, Munnar.
